summary: American Precisionist painter from Lancaster, Pennsylvania. Best known for I Saw the Figure 5 in Gold (inspired by William Carlos Williams poem). Created 'poster portraits' of friends. Also painted My Egypt, watercolor illustrations of Henry James. Associated with hard-edged American modernism.

works.name: I Saw the Figure 5 in Gold (1928)
works.description: Poster portrait inspired by William Carlos Williams's poem 'The Great Figure.' Features the number 5 on a fire truck with the word 'BILL' and diagonal light beams. Wikipedia

images.caption: I Saw the Figure 5 in Gold

clues.clue: The word 'BILL' appears above an abstract fire truck inspired by a William Carlos Williams poem; the title numeral appears in gold
examples: the name 'BILL' appears above an abstract fire truck inspired by a William Carlos Williams poem
placed the fragmented word 'BILL' in the upper left of a painting that features scattered streetlights and a red shape resembling the fire truck

works.name: My Egypt
works.description: Painting of grain elevators in Lancaster, Pennsylvania, with beams of light, linking industrial architecture to Egyptian pyramids.

clues.clue: Grain elevators with diagonal light beams in Lancaster, Pennsylvania; links industrial buildings to Egyptian pyramids
examples: used diagonal, fragmented planes to suggest an inspirational, spiritual dimension of a structure, thereby linking it with the pyramids
intersecting bands of light in front of a large grain elevator

works.name: Poster Portraits
works.description: Series of nine 'poster portraits' of friends. Includes portrait of Arthur Dove with a scythe and the word 'DOVE.' I Saw the Figure 5 in Gold is a poster portrait of Williams.

clues.clue: Created nine 'poster portraits' of friends; one shows a scythe and the word 'DOVE' for Arthur Dove
examples: created 'poster portraits' of his friends include one with the word 'Bill' on it
painted a portrait of Arthur Dove showing a giant scythe dominating a people-less landscape where the word 'Dove' appears
portrayed Lancaster, Pennsylvania in his works

clues.clue: Painted watercolor illustrations of Henry James stories (The Beast in the Jungle, The Turn of the Screw)
examples: Henry James's The Beast in the Jungle inspired this artist to paint the watercolor Boat Ride from Sorrento

clues.clue: Homosexuality portrayed in Turkish Bath with Self Portrait
examples: homosexuality was portrayed in his work Turkish Bath with Self Portrait

comprehensive_summary:
Charles Demuth (1883-1935) was an American Precisionist painter from Lancaster, Pennsylvania. His most famous work is I Saw the Figure 5 in Gold (1928), a poster portrait inspired by William Carlos Williams's poem 'The Great Figure,' featuring the number 5 on an abstract fire truck with the word 'BILL' and diagonal light beams.

My Egypt depicts grain elevators in Lancaster with diagonal light beams, linking industrial architecture to Egyptian pyramids. He created nine 'poster portraits' of friends, including one of Arthur Dove showing a scythe and the word 'DOVE.' He also painted watercolor illustrations of Henry James stories. His homosexuality was portrayed in Turkish Bath with Self Portrait.
